name: Orange Chicken Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb chicken breast (boneless, skinless), cut into strips
3 cups mixed fresh vegetables (such as green bell pepper, red bell pepper, snow peas, carrots, green onions, mushrooms, and/or onions)
1/2 cup unsalted cashew bits OR cashew halves
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine orange juice, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, sherry, ginger, orange peel, and garlic in a large glass bowl.
2. Add chicken to the marinade and let it sit in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
3. Drain the chicken; reserve the marinade.
4. Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
5. Add the chicken to the skillet and stir-fry for 3 minutes, or until the chicken is light brown.
6. Add the mixed vegetables and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, or until the vegetables are crisp-tender.
7. Combine cornstarch with the reserved marinade in a separate bowl.
8. Pour the mixture into the skillet and stir until thickened.
9. Stir in cashews and cook for an additional minute.
10. Serve over hot cooked rice.
Each serving provides:
590 calories
48 g. protein
20 g. fat
4 g. saturated fat
53 g. carbohydrate
4 g. dietary fiber
109 mg. cholesterol
822 mg. sodium
Source: Rice & Chicken - New Ideas for Old Favorites
